req.title: Export employee directory — variant 2
req.statement: An hr-admin shall export the employee directory as CSV with PII redacted for employees who opted out of public directory. Additionally, the action must be idempotent under retry.

doc.reasoning: R-WEB-083 defines four testable concerns: (1) happy-path CSV export with PII redaction for opted-out employees, (2) access control rejection for non-hr-admin roles, (3) idempotency under retry, and (4) rate-limit enforcement. EP covers the valid hr-admin partition; NEG covers the forbidden-role and rate-limit error paths; BVA covers the idempotency boundary (two identical sequential requests must return identical content); DT covers the redaction decision table (opted-in vs opted-out employees in the same export). A fifth test uses DT to validate both redacted and unredacted rows coexist correctly.

tests.name: test_hr_admin_export_returns_csv_with_pii_redacted
preconditions: At least one employee has opted out of public directory
At least one employee has not opted out
actions: Authenticate as hr-admin and obtain bearer token
Parse the streamed CSV response body
expected: HTTP 200 with Content-Type text/csv or application/octet-stream
CSV contains a row for each opted-out employee with PII fields replaced by REDACTED or empty
CSV contains full PII for employees who have not opted out
tests.executable:
import csv, io
import pytest

def test_hr_admin_export_returns_csv_with_pii_redacted(
    hr_admin_token, api_client, opted_out_employee, opted_in_employee
):
    resp = api_client.get(
        "/api/employees/export.csv",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {hr_admin_token}"},
        stream=True,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    ct = resp.headers.get("Content-Type", "")
    assert "csv" in ct or "octet-stream" in ct

    reader = csv.DictReader(io.StringIO(resp.text))
    rows = list(reader)
    assert rows, "CSV must not be empty"

    ids = {r["employee_id"]: r for r in rows}

    out_row = ids.get(str(opted_out_employee["id"]))
    assert out_row is not None, "Opted-out employee must appear in export"
    assert out_row["email"] in ("", "REDACTED"), "PII must be redacted"
    assert out_row["phone"] in ("", "REDACTED"), "PII must be redacted"

    in_row = ids.get(str(opted_in_employee["id"]))
    assert in_row is not None, "Opted-in employee must appear in export"
    assert in_row["email"] == opted_in_employee["email"], "PII must be present"

tests.name: test_non_hr_admin_export_returns_403
actions: Authenticate as a regular employee and obtain bearer token
expected: Response body contains an error message indicating insufficient permissions
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_non_hr_admin_export_returns_403(employee_token, api_client):
    resp = api_client.get(
        "/api/employees/export.csv",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {employee_token}"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 403
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "message" in body, "Error payload expected"

tests.name: test_export_is_idempotent_under_retry
preconditions: Employee directory is stable (no changes between calls)
actions: Authenticate as hr-admin and obtain bearer token
expected: Both response bodies are byte-for-byte identical (idempotency)
trace: R-WEB-083: action must be idempotent under retry

tests.name: test_rate_limited_export_returns_429
preconditions: Rate-limit threshold for /api/employees/export.csv is known (e.g. N requests/minute)
actions: Authenticate as hr-admin and obtain bearer token
Issue N+1 rapid successive GET /api/employees/export.csv requests
expected: The (N+1)th request returns HTTP 429 Too Many Requests
Response contains Retry-After header or equivalent

tests.name: test_export_redaction_decision_table_opted_in_and_opted_out
preconditions: Directory contains exactly two employees: one opted-in, one opted-out
actions: Parse CSV and evaluate each row against its opt-out status
expected: Opted-in employee row: email and phone match source data (no redaction)
Opted-out employee row: email and phone are REDACTED or blank
Both employees appear in the CSV (no rows omitted)
Non-PII columns (e.g. department, employee_id) are present and unredacted for both rows
tests.executable:
import csv, io
import pytest

PII_FIELDS = ["email", "phone"]
NON_PII_FIELDS = ["employee_id", "department"]

def test_export_redaction_decision_table_opted_in_and_opted_out(
    hr_admin_token, api_client, opted_out_employee, opted_in_employee
):
    resp = api_client.get(
        "/api/employees/export.csv",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {hr_admin_token}"},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200
    rows = {r["employee_id"]: r for r in csv.DictReader(io.StringIO(resp.text))}

    out_id = str(opted_out_employee["id"])
    in_id = str(opted_in_employee["id"])
    assert out_id in rows and in_id in rows, "Both employees must be in CSV"

    for field in PII_FIELDS:
        assert rows[out_id][field] in ("", "REDACTED"), f"{field} must be redacted"
        assert rows[in_id][field] == opted_in_employee[field], f"{field} must be intact"

    for field in NON_PII_FIELDS:
        assert rows[out_id][field], f"Non-PII {field} missing for opted-out employee"
        assert rows[in_id][field], f"Non-PII {field} missing for opted-in employee"
